sections.paragraph:
- Original: ὑάκινθος

- Transliteration: Huakinthos

- Phonetic: hoo-ak'-in-thos

- Definition:

1. hyacinth, the name of a flower, also of a precious stone of the same colour, a dark blue verging on black

- Origin: of uncertain derivation

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Noun Masculine

- Strong's: Of uncertain derivation; the hyacinth or jacinth that is some gem of a deep blue color probably the zirkon: - jacinth.
